title: Fitness Predicts Death Better Than Smoking
description: Cardiorespiratory fitness — measurable, trainable, and almost entirely ignored in routine clinical care — turns out to be a stronger predictor of early death than smoking, hypertension, or diabetes. This piece examines what the evidence from over 2.3 million adults actually shows, why the American Heart Association called for VO2 max to be treated as a vital sign eight years ago and almost nobody listened, and what the dementia data from a 44-year follow-up study means for the decisions you make in your forties.
